rationale: Option B is coherently implemented at 50165d81. R1's four detection concerns are internally consistent: the worktree-enum leg is explicitly ADDED (probe leg and shared predicate byte-unchanged for complete/next/doctor), fail-CLOSED on WorktreeList/ancestry error, epic-scoped via the same closed-bead set, and never consults branchExistsFn — so it cannot contradict never-false-refuse (a cleanly-deleted branch has no worktree entry; git forbids deleting a worktree-checked-out branch, and false-refusal falsifiers are correctly scoped to legitimate absence, with infra-error refusal the sanctioned asymmetry per OQ2). The honest residual holds: Goal discloses THREE residuals mapping one-to-one to Non-Goals; the round-6 race is CLOSED BY DESIGN (not 'implausible', not an audited-refutation carry); Fact 2 re-scoped to PERSISTENT corruption with the temporal gap explicitly conceded in R1; the two-List() window is disclosed and serialization-bounded, and the future single-snapshot hardening coexists with OQ4's no-seam resolution 'for THIS spec'. Verified empirically: both round-7 test names zero Go hits at HEAD; bead.WorktreeList at bdcli.go:202-217 returns ([]WorktreeListEntry, error); impl.go:13 already imports internal/bead. All 4 OQs [x]; scope still ~2-3 beads; internal/bead correctly declared read-only-consumed.

findings.item: Worktree-enumeration merge-prevention leg added to R1 (not replacing; fail-closed; epic-scoped; shared predicate unchanged)
findings.note: R1 states 'ADDITIONALLY enumerates', 'REMAINS for breadth', 'Option B only ADDS a gate leg'; leg never consults branchExistsFn; fail-closed on bead.WorktreeList()/IsAncestor error with the merge-SKIPPING FinalizeEpic asymmetry explained; epic-scoped by the same bd list --parent closed-bead set so cross-spec worktrees neither trigger nor suppress (blp6 unchanged). Grounding verified: bdcli.go:202-217 cleanly signals errors; approve imports bead at impl.go:13 (ADR-0030-clean, no executor git-IO).

findings.item: Honest residual: round-6 race closed by design, Fact 2 re-scoped to persistent, two-List() residual disclosed, Goal discloses three residuals
findings.note: Goal's three residuals map one-to-one to Non-Goals bullets (raw-merge-no-obligation, blp6 second residual, two-List() window). Non-Goals says the race 'is not claimed implausible, and it is no longer carried as an audited-refutation residual'; R1 concedes 'Facts 1&2 do NOT close the temporal gap'. Fact 2 now says PERSISTENTLY masked in both R1 and Non-Goals. Single-snapshot handoff noted as future hardening without contradicting OQ4 (no-seam 'stands for THIS spec'). Headline coverage claim hedged ('wherever its detection signal or a recorded obligation can see the gap') — no overclaim.

Test name zero Go-code hits at 50165d81 (verified) — RED-at-bcb255eb claim honest. Subcases (a)/(b)/(c) mirror R1's leg semantics exactly, including the RED-on-revert-to-probe-only discriminator and the ancestor + different-spec false-positive exclusions. Falsified-if bullets in R1 cover the same three cases — AC and requirement in lockstep.

findings.item: AC10 upgraded from pre-applied grep -l to RED-on-revert TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant
findings.note: Test name zero Go-code hits at 50165d81 (verified). Exactly-one-workflow-claimant invariant parses ALL domain OWNERSHIP.yaml so a third-domain claim also fails; the former shell one-liner correctly demoted to a non-discriminating regression pin in Validation Proofs. Consistent with the Impacted Domains workflow claim and AC10's docs/ownership-bead placement.

findings.item: No-false-refuse coherence of the enum leg vs the deleted-branch normal path
findings.note: No contradiction: a cleanly-deleted branch has no worktree entry on the normal path (complete cleans branch+worktree; git refuses deleting a checked-out worktree branch), so neither leg triggers — AC2(d) intact. An infra-noise IsAncestor error on an enumerated worktree refuses, which is the sanctioned fail-closed-on-infra asymmetry OQ2 states applies 'to INFRA errors, never to legitimate absence'. The leg's always-runnable complete recovery is truthful (enumerated worktree implies existing branch) and does not conflict with R3's branch-less distinct recourse.

All 4 OQs [x]; OQ2's rounds-2..7 narrative matches R1 and Non-Goals precisely (probe stays bool, Facts 1&2 AC11/AC12, round-7 leg AC13, serialization-bounded residual). Scope bullets updated coherently: enum leg in internal/approve only, shared predicate gains no worktree awareness, no internal/bead or gitutil change lands, internal/bead declared read-only-consumed in Impacted Domains. Still ~2-3 beads. blp6 Non-Goals constraint correctly extended to protect the same-source enumeration invariant AC13 depends on.
